User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the application, storing core profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

displayName.description: The display name of the user.

photoURL.description: URL of the user's profile photo.

creationTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

lastSignInTime.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user signed in.

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user has administrative privileges.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores additional profile information for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile entity.

bio.description: A short biography or description of the user.

website.description: URL of the user's personal website.

definition.description: Stores user account information. Path-based ownership simplifies security rules, ensuring only the user can access their data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores additional profile information for a user. Path-based ownership ensures data privacy.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: profileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user profile.

firestore.reasoning:
Based on the provided entities and the application's core features, a simple and secure Firestore structure is recommended, focusing on user-centric data organization. This design prioritizes Authorization Independence, QAPs, and structural segregation to ensure robust security rules and efficient data access.

1.  **User Data:** User profiles and related data are stored under `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership simplifies security rules, making it straightforward to control access based on the user's ID. The UserProfile entity is placed as a subcollection of `/users/{userId}/profiles/{profileId}`. This approach ensures that only the user can access their profile data, enforcing a private data model.

*   **Authorization Independence:** The design avoids `get()` calls in security rules by directly associating user data with the user's ID in the path.
*   **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Because data is segregated by user ID, list operations are inherently secure. Listing profiles under a user ID can be done securely without filtering.
*   **Structural Segregation:** All documents under `/users/{userId}` share the same security posture, simplifying the rules and making them more maintainable.
